mission.title: Install and Configure Charmed Kubernetes
mission.description: Charmed Kubernetes is a distribution of Kubernetes that is tested across various clouds and focuses on modern metrics and monitoring. It is ideal for enterprises looking for a robust Kubernetes solution on Ubuntu. Key trade-offs include the need for Juju for deployment and management, which may add complexity for some users.

steps.title: Install Juju
steps.description:
Juju is required to deploy Charmed Kubernetes. This command installs Juju on your system.

```bash

sudo snap install juju --classic

```

> Ensure you have snap installed on your system.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Kubernetes Controller
steps.description:
This command initializes the Juju controller, which manages the deployment of Charmed Kubernetes.

```bash

juju bootstrap localhost k8s-controller --config controller-image=ubuntu:22.04

```

> Replace 'localhost' with your cloud provider if needed.

steps.title: Deploy Charmed Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Charmed Kubernetes bundle, which includes all necessary components.

```bash

juju deploy charmed-kubernetes

```

> This will deploy the latest version. Specify a version if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the deployed services to ensure everything is running correctly.

```bash

juju status

```

> Look for all services to be in the 'active' state.

steps.title: Post-Installation Configuration
steps.description:
Configure additional features like monitoring and autoscaling as needed.

```bash

juju config kube-api enable-admission-plugins=PodSecurityPolicy

```

> Example configuration for enabling admission plugins.

uninstall.title: Remove Charmed Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Charmed Kubernetes deployment. Be aware that this will delete all associated data and resources.

```bash

juju destroy-controller k8s-controller --destroy-all-models

```

> This will also remove orphaned resources.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Juju
uninstall.description:
This command removes any remaining Juju configurations and data.

```bash

sudo snap remove juju

```

> This will uninstall Juju from your system.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Charmed Kubernetes
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your current configuration and data. This command upgrades the Charmed Kubernetes deployment.

```bash

juju upgrade-charm charmed-kubernetes

```

> Check the Juju documentation for specific upgrade flags.

upgrade.title: Verify the Upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, check the status of the services to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

juju status

```

> Ensure all services are in the 'active' state after the upgrade.

upgrade.title: Rollback if Necessary
upgrade.description:
If the upgrade fails, you can rollback to the previous version.

```bash

juju rollback charmed-kubernetes

```

> This will revert to the last successful deployment.

troubleshooting.title: juju bootstrap fails with 'unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Juju controller cannot reach the cloud provider.

**Fix:**
Check your network settings and ensure the cloud provider is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: juju deploy charmed-kubernetes hangs indefinitely
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are insufficient resources available in the cloud.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cloud instance has at least 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM available.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get nodes returns 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes nodes are not properly initialized.

**Fix:**
Run 'juju status' to check for issues with the deployment and resolve any errors.

troubleshooting.title: juju status shows services in 'error' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to misconfiguration or failed charm deployments.

**Fix:**
Review the logs for the specific service using 'juju debug-log' and address any issues.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.31 features, including improved metrics server integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecation of certain legacy charms that are no longer maintained.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new configuration options for metrics.

resolution.summary: You should see 'active' status for all services when running 'juju status'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary resources and tools installed before proceeding.
